overview.brief_description: Szeged, known as the 'City of Sunshine,' is a vibrant cultural and university city located in southern Hungary near the Romanian and Serbian borders. Renowned for its stunning Art Nouveau architecture and lively festivals, Szeged offers a perfect blend of history, gastronomy, and natural beauty along the Tisza River.
why_visit: Explore magnificent Art Nouveau buildings and the iconic Votive Church
Experience the annual Szeged Open-Air Festival featuring theatre and opera
Enjoy delicious local cuisine, especially the famous Szeged fish soup
Relax along the scenic Tisza River and in the city's numerous parks
overview.best_time_to_visit: May to September, when the weather is warm and festivals are in full swing

seasonal_notes.spring: Mild temperatures with blooming parks and the start of outdoor events
seasonal_notes.summer: Hot and sunny, ideal for river activities and attending the Open-Air Festival
seasonal_notes.autumn: Pleasant weather with beautiful fall foliage and harvest celebrations
seasonal_notes.winter: Cool and quieter, offering a peaceful atmosphere and festive Christmas markets

practical_info.visit_duration: 2 to 3 days to explore key sights and enjoy local culture
practical_info.getting_around: Walking, cycling, and a reliable public bus network
practical_info.safety: Generally safe for tourists with standard urban precautions recommended

must_do: Visit the Szeged Votive Church and climb its towers for panoramic views
Stroll through Dugonics Square and admire its historic architecture
Attend a performance at the Szeged National Theatre
Sample authentic Szeged fish soup at a local restaurant
Relax in the Szeged Botanical Garden and the nearby parks
worth_doing: Explore the Móra Ferenc Museum to learn about regional history
Take a boat trip or walk along the Tisza River promenade
Visit the Reök Palace to see impressive Art Nouveau exhibitions
Experience local life at Szeged Market Hall
Discover the University of Szeged campus and its lively atmosphere
hidden_gems: Discover the charming inner courtyards around the city center
Visit the Szeged Synagogue, a beautiful example of historic architecture
Explore the small galleries and artisan shops in the Fekete Ház area
Relax at the Anna Thermal Bath for a traditional Hungarian spa experience
Attend a local wine tasting event featuring wines from the Szeged region

sites.name: Votive Church of Szeged (Szegedi Dóm)
sites.description: The Votive Church is a stunning example of Neo-Romanesque architecture, dominating Szeged's cityscape with its twin towers and beautiful mosaics. Built to commemorate the city's recovery from the great flood of 1879, it is a symbol of resilience and faith, featuring intricate stained glass windows and a grand organ.
sites.booking_tips: Free entry; guided tours available during peak season

opening_hours.notes: Hours may vary on religious holidays

sites.name: Szeged National Theater
sites.description: Szeged National Theater is a key cultural venue hosting opera, ballet, and drama performances. The modern architecture blends with artistic interiors, making it a vibrant center for performing arts in the city.
sites.seasonal_notes: Seasonal programming varies; check schedule for festivals
sites.booking_tips: Book tickets online in advance for popular shows

sites.name: Anna Thermal Bath and Wellness
sites.description: Anna Thermal Bath offers a relaxing experience with thermal pools, saunas, and wellness treatments sourced from natural hot springs. This historic spa has been a favorite for locals seeking rejuvenation since the early 20th century.
sites.seasonal_notes: Saunas and indoor pools available year-round
sites.booking_tips: Reserve spa treatments in advance, especially weekends

sites.name: Szeged Zoo
sites.description: Szeged Zoo houses a diverse collection of animals from around the world, focusing on conservation and education. The zoo's naturalistic enclosures and interactive exhibits make it a great family destination.
sites.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; some animal exhibits indoors for winter
sites.booking_tips: Tickets available online with discounts for families

sites.name: Dömötör Tower
sites.description: The Dömötör Tower is the oldest building in Szeged, dating back to the 12th century. This medieval tower served as a bell tower and watchtower and offers insight into the city's early history and architecture.
sites.seasonal_notes: Open seasonally, usually spring to autumn
sites.booking_tips: Check opening days in advance; guided tours available locally

sites.name: Reök Palace
sites.description: Reök Palace is an Art Nouveau masterpiece housing a contemporary art gallery. Its ornate facade and elegant interiors create a unique atmosphere for exhibitions showcasing Hungarian and international artists.
sites.booking_tips: Check exhibition schedule online before visiting

sites.name: Szeged Synagogue
sites.description: The Szeged Synagogue is a beautiful blend of Art Nouveau and Moorish architectural styles, reflecting the city's Jewish heritage. It remains active and offers guided tours that highlight its art, history, and community importance.
sites.booking_tips: Arrange guided tours in advance, especially for groups

sites.name: Szeged Open-air Festival
sites.description: Held annually in summer, the Szeged Open-air Festival is one of Hungary's most famous cultural events featuring operas, musicals, and theatrical performances in the iconic Votive Church square. It creates a magical atmosphere under the stars with world-class productions.
sites.booking_tips: Book tickets months in advance, especially for popular shows

visit_profile.weather_fallback: No indoor alternative for open-air shows

sites.name: Kárász Street
sites.description: Kárász Street is Szeged’s bustling pedestrian zone filled with shops, cafes, and historic architecture. It is perfect for a leisurely stroll to enjoy local flavors, street performances, and the vibrant atmosphere of the city center.

visit_profile.weather_fallback: Visit indoor cafes or galleries nearby

sites.name: Déli Park
sites.description: Déli Park is a peaceful green space ideal for relaxing, picnics, and outdoor activities. With well-maintained paths and playgrounds, it is a favorite spot for families and locals, offering a calm retreat from the urban buzz.
sites.seasonal_notes: Beautiful in spring with blooming flowers

visit_profile.weather_fallback: Visit nearby Szeged Zoo indoor exhibits

sites.name: Pick Salami and Szeged Paprika Museum
sites.description: Dedicated to Szeged’s iconic salami and paprika products, this museum showcases the history and production of these local culinary treasures. Visitors can see traditional manufacturing methods and taste authentic flavors.
sites.booking_tips: Guided tours recommended for full experience

sites.name: Móra Ferenc Museum
sites.description: The Móra Ferenc Museum offers an extensive collection of archaeology, ethnography, and natural history exhibits focused on the Csongrád region. It is named after the famous Hungarian writer and provides great insight into local culture and history.
sites.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; special exhibitions in summer

sites.name: Széchenyi Square
sites.description: Széchenyi Square is the central square of Szeged, surrounded by historic buildings, cafes, and the impressive City Hall. It is a popular meeting point and hosts various events, markets, and open-air concerts throughout the year.
sites.seasonal_notes: Christmas market and summer festivals held here

visit_profile.weather_fallback: Visit indoor cafes or museums nearby

sites.name: Szeged Market Hall (Vásárcsarnok)
sites.description: The Szeged Market Hall is a vibrant indoor market featuring fresh produce, local delicacies, and artisan crafts. Its historic architecture and bustling atmosphere provide an authentic taste of daily life in Szeged.
sites.seasonal_notes: Best visits in spring and summer for fresh seasonal products
sites.booking_tips: No booking required; early mornings are less crowded

sites.name: Tisza River Promenade
sites.description: The Tisza River Promenade offers scenic walking paths along the riverbanks, perfect for cycling, jogging, or relaxing with views of the water and city skyline. It is especially beautiful at sunset and during spring blossoms.
sites.seasonal_notes: Best visited in spring and summer; some paths muddy in heavy rain

sites.name: Szent István Square
sites.description: Szent István Square is a charming historic square featuring statues, cafes, and access to several important buildings including the Dömötör Tower. It is ideal for soaking in Szeged’s atmosphere and enjoying local street life.
sites.seasonal_notes: Seasonal events and markets take place here

sites.name: University of Szeged Botanical Garden
sites.description: The University Botanical Garden features a wide variety of native and exotic plants in beautifully landscaped settings. It serves educational and conservation purposes and offers a tranquil environment for nature lovers.
sites.seasonal_notes: Best in spring and early summer when flowers bloom
sites.booking_tips: Entry tickets available on site; group tours possible by arrangement

neighborhoods.name: Belváros
neighborhoods.character: Belváros pulses with the vibrant energy of Szeged’s heart, where historic charm meets bustling city life. Its lively streets are lined with cafés, shops, and cultural venues, making it the city’s main hub for visitors and locals alike.

location.description: Located in the very center of Szeged along the Tisza River.

history.overview: Belváros developed around Szeged’s medieval core and was extensively rebuilt after the great 1879 flood.
history.significance: It is the historic and cultural heart of the city, showcasing iconic architecture and public spaces.
notable_events: 1879 Great Flood and subsequent city reconstruction

practical_info.safety: Generally safe with regular police presence
practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Late spring to early autumn for festivals and outdoor dining

attractions.name: Votive Church
attractions.description: An iconic neo-Romanesque cathedral dominating Szeged’s skyline.
attractions.appeal: Its stunning architecture and panoramic bell tower views attract visitors year-round.

attractions.name: Dóm Square
attractions.description: Central square hosting the famous Szeged Open-Air Festival each summer.
attractions.appeal: A cultural hotspot combining outdoor performances with beautiful surroundings.

insider_tips: Visit early morning to enjoy the square before crowds arrive.
Try the local fish soup at a riverside café.
Attend an event at the Open-Air Theatre during summer.

neighborhoods.name: Felsőváros
neighborhoods.character: Felsőváros is a trendy, youthful neighborhood brimming with creative energy and modern flair. Hip cafés, boutique shops, and street art create a vibrant atmosphere favored by students and artists.

location.description: Northwest of the city center, about a 10-minute tram ride from Belváros.

history.overview: Originally a residential district, it evolved into a cultural hotspot in recent decades.
history.significance: Home to Szeged’s artistic institutions and a center for contemporary culture.

practical_info.safety: Safe with a lively street presence
practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Spring and summer for outdoor terraces and festivals

attractions.name: Reök Palace
attractions.description: A stunning Art Nouveau villa hosting modern art exhibitions.
attractions.appeal: Architectural beauty combined with dynamic art programming.

insider_tips: Explore the side streets to discover local street art.
Visit the Reök Palace during evening openings for a unique experience.
Try the craft beers at one of the local microbreweries.

neighborhoods.name: Fodorka
neighborhoods.character: Fodorka is the main food district of Szeged, where local markets and diverse eateries merge into a feast for the senses. The neighborhood buzzes with vendors selling fresh produce, spices, and traditional ingredients alongside modern restaurants.

location.description: Just east of Belváros, adjacent to the Tisza River.

history.overview: Historically Szeged’s commercial hub for food trade and markets.
history.significance: It remains the culinary heart where traditions meet contemporary gastronomy.
notable_events: Establishment of Szeged Market Hall in 1904

practical_info.safety: Safe and lively during market hours
practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning to early afternoon for markets

attractions.name: Szeged Market Hall
attractions.description: A historic indoor market offering fresh produce, spices, and local specialties.
attractions.appeal: An immersive experience into Szeged’s culinary traditions.

dining.name: Halászcsárda
dining.known_for: Traditional Szeged fish soup and river fish dishes

insider_tips: Visit early to catch the freshest market stalls.
Try the homemade paprika products unique to Szeged.
Combine a market visit with a riverside walk along the Tisza.

neighborhoods.name: Rókus
neighborhoods.character: Rókus is a quiet, primarily residential neighborhood known for its tree-lined streets and a relaxed suburban feel. It offers a peaceful contrast to the busy city center with parks and local shops serving daily needs.

location.description: Southeast of Belváros, on the opposite side of the Tisza River.

history.overview: Historically a workers’ district that expanded with the city’s industrial growth.
history.significance: Represents the everyday life and community spirit of Szeged’s residents.

practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Year-round for quiet walks and local markets
practical_info.walkability: Moderately walkable with some green spaces

attractions.name: Szent Anna Square
attractions.description: A calm green space ideal for relaxation and community events.
attractions.appeal: Offers a glimpse of everyday life away from tourist crowds.

insider_tips: Visit the local market on weekends for fresh produce.
Take a peaceful walk along the riverbank paths.
Try a coffee and cake at Tóth Cukrászda to experience local flavors.

neighborhoods.name: Alsóváros
neighborhoods.character: Alsóváros is historically rich with charming streets and venerable buildings, offering a quiet stroll through Szeged’s past. Its cozy cafés and small galleries attract visitors who appreciate history blended with a calm ambiance.

location.description: Southwest of Belváros, close to the river and main train station.

history.overview: One of Szeged’s oldest neighborhoods, with roots going back to medieval times.
history.significance: Preserves architectural heritage and cultural institutions.

practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Spring and autumn for pleasant weather
practical_info.walkability: Very walkable with many pedestrian areas

attractions.name: Szeged Synagogue
attractions.description: A beautiful 19th-century synagogue known for its Moorish Revival architecture.
attractions.appeal: A key site for Jewish heritage in the region.

attractions.name: National Theater of Szeged
attractions.description: A cultural venue offering diverse theatrical performances in a historic building.
attractions.appeal: Showcases Hungary’s vibrant performing arts scene.

dining.name: Kertész Restaurant
dining.known_for: Hearty traditional dishes and local wines

insider_tips: Check the National Theater schedule for a live performance.
Visit the synagogue with a guided tour to appreciate its history.
Walk the quiet streets in the early evening for a peaceful experience.

neighborhoods.name: Tarján
neighborhoods.character: Tarján is a growing residential area with a mix of modern housing and green spaces, appealing to families and those seeking a suburban lifestyle close to the city. It offers parks and sports facilities balancing urban convenience with outdoor activities.

location.description: Northwest outskirts of Szeged, beyond Felsőváros.

history.overview: Originally a village absorbed into Szeged’s urban expansion during the 20th century.
history.significance: Represents modern growth and suburban development in Szeged.
notable_events: Development of sports facilities in the 2000s

practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Summer for park activities and sports events

attractions.name: Tarján Park
attractions.description: A large green area featuring playgrounds and walking trails.
attractions.appeal: Ideal for family outings and outdoor sports.

insider_tips: Rent a bike to explore the extensive park areas.
Visit during local sports events to experience community spirit.
Try homemade pastries at the nearby bakery.

fine_dining.name: Bordó Szem Bistro
fine_dining.description: Elegant bistro offering modern takes on Hungarian classics with a refined atmosphere.
fine_dining.atmosphere: Chic and intimate, perfect for special occasions.
fine_dining.local_tips: Try the Szegedi halászlé, a spicy fish soup, their signature dish.

fine_dining.name: Bisztró 28
fine_dining.description: Modern bistro with creative dishes highlighting local ingredients and seasonal produce.
fine_dining.atmosphere: Trendy yet relaxed, great for food enthusiasts.
fine_dining.local_tips: Reserve ahead on weekends to secure a table, and don't miss the Szegedi paprika chicken.

fine_dining.name: Kikelet Étterem
fine_dining.description: Classic Hungarian dishes served in a refined setting, focusing on authentic flavors.
fine_dining.atmosphere: Classic and elegant with a cozy ambiance.
fine_dining.local_tips: Their fish soup with carp is a must-try during colder months.

casual_dining.name: Pick Salami and Szeged Paprika Museum Café
casual_dining.description: Casual spot attached to the museum serving traditional Szeged sausage sandwiches and paprika-inspired dishes.
casual_dining.atmosphere: Casual and lively with a cultural touch.
casual_dining.local_tips: Try the Pick salami platter paired with local bread.

casual_dining.name: Tiszavirág Restaurant
casual_dining.description: Comfortable restaurant offering hearty Hungarian dishes alongside international favorites.
casual_dining.atmosphere: Friendly and warm, great for groups and families.
casual_dining.local_tips: Their duck leg with red cabbage is highly recommended.

casual_dining.name: Körséta Étterem
casual_dining.description: A local favorite offering traditional dishes with a modern twist in a relaxed setting.
casual_dining.atmosphere: Casual and welcoming with local charm.
casual_dining.local_tips: Ask for the seasonal fish dishes featuring Tisza river fish.

casual_dining.name: Fekete Gólya Kávézó & Étterem
casual_dining.description: A cozy spot blending café vibes with a restaurant serving homemade Hungarian favorites.
casual_dining.atmosphere: Relaxed and artsy, perfect for casual dining or coffee breaks.
casual_dining.local_tips: Try their homemade desserts and Hungarian pastries.

casual_dining.name: Gourmet Fodrász
casual_dining.description: Popular spot for quick Hungarian street food with a focus on quality ingredients.
casual_dining.atmosphere: Casual and vibrant, perfect for a quick meal.
casual_dining.local_tips: Their lángos with sour cream and cheese is a local favorite.

street_food.name: Szeged Market Hall
street_food.description: The main indoor market offering a variety of local produce and street food stalls featuring traditional Hungarian snacks.
street_food.local_tips: Try the kürtőskalács (chimney cake) from the market stalls.

street_food.name: Kígyó Street Food Festival (seasonal event)
street_food.description: Annual street food festival held in summer with a variety of vendors serving both traditional and fusion dishes.
street_food.local_tips: Visit in summer to enjoy live music alongside street food.

street_food.name: Dóm Square Food Stalls
street_food.description: Food stalls around Szeged’s iconic Dóm Square offering quick traditional snacks and drinks.
street_food.local_tips: Try the spicy Szegedi fish soup served in bread bowls.

bars.name: Dóm Udvar
bars.description: Stylish bar with a great selection of Hungarian wines and cocktails in a cozy courtyard setting near the cathedral.
bars.atmosphere: Intimate and relaxed with a sophisticated vibe.
bars.local_tips: Try local Szeged wines and enjoy the view of the cathedral.

bars.name: Vagyok Bár
bars.description: Trendy bar with a creative cocktail menu and a lively atmosphere popular among locals.
bars.atmosphere: Energetic and modern, great for nights out.
bars.local_tips: Ask the bartenders for a custom cocktail based on your taste.

bars.name: Múzeum Café & Bar
bars.description: A hybrid café and bar offering great coffee by day and craft beers by night, set near Szeged's cultural center.
bars.atmosphere: Casual and artsy with a young crowd.
bars.local_tips: Try their Hungarian craft beers paired with light snacks.

cafes.name: Déryné Bisztró & Café
cafes.description: Classic café known for its pastries, coffee, and relaxed atmosphere in the city center.
cafes.atmosphere: Cozy and historic, ideal for afternoon breaks.
cafes.local_tips: Try the traditional Hungarian cakes, especially the Dobos torte.

cafes.name: Kávéház Szeged
cafes.description: Elegant café serving high-quality coffee, teas, and light bites in a refined setting.
cafes.atmosphere: Elegant and quiet, perfect for meetings or relaxed mornings.
cafes.local_tips: Pair your coffee with their fresh strudel options.

cafes.name: Madal Café Szeged
cafes.description: Known for expertly brewed specialty coffee and friendly service in a minimalist space.
cafes.atmosphere: Modern and minimalist, great for coffee aficionados.

food_experiences.name: Szeged Culinary Walking Tour
food_experiences.description: Guided walking tour exploring Szeged’s food markets, local eateries, and iconic dishes like Szegedi halászlé.
food_experiences.local_tips: Book in advance and wear comfortable shoes for walking.

food_experiences.name: Paprika and Pick Salami Museum Visit & Tasting
food_experiences.description: Experience Hungary’s signature paprika and famous Szeged salami with interactive tastings and museum tours.
food_experiences.location: Pick Salami and Szeged Paprika Museum
food_experiences.local_tips: Combine the visit with a meal at the museum café.

food_experiences.name: Hungarian Cooking Class in Szeged
food_experiences.description: Hands-on cooking classes focused on traditional Szeged dishes such as fish soup and paprika chicken.
food_experiences.local_tips: Great as a group activity; book several weeks in advance.

food_experiences.name: Seasonal Market Visit with Local Producer Tastings
food_experiences.description: Explore local farmers’ markets with a guide, sampling seasonal produce, cheeses, and cured meats unique to the Szeged region.
food_experiences.location: Szeged Market Hall and outdoor markets
food_experiences.local_tips: Visit early morning for the freshest selections.

spring: Fresh asparagus dishes from local markets
Early spring lamb stew with Hungarian herbs
Seasonal fruit preserves featuring cherries and apricots
summer: Szegedi halászlé (spicy fish soup) served cold or hot
Fresh fruit desserts with local peaches and berries
autumn: Mushroom dishes featuring local forest varieties
Game meat stews with root vegetables
winter: Hearty fish soups with carp from the Tisza River
Traditional Hungarian Christmas pastries like bejgli

transport_types.notes: Direct InterCity trains available, comfortable and scenic route.
Frequent buses from Szeged bus station to Budapest Népliget.
Requires transfer in Budapest; comfortable international trains.
Direct buses available, often cheaper than trains.
Direct international buses from Szeged to Belgrade.
Direct buses; convenient for connecting to Bratislava city center.
Flight from nearby Budapest Airport; must travel to Budapest first.
Multiple transfers, including Budapest and Vienna.
Direct long-distance bus, comfortable and economical.
Direct buses available with comfortable seating.
Requires transfer in Budapest or Osijek.
Direct bus connection, economical for budget travelers.
Requires transfer in Budapest; scenic routes.
Flight from Budapest Airport; must travel to Budapest first.
Long-distance overnight buses with border crossing.
Direct long-distance bus; overnight options available.
Requires transfers, typically via Budapest and Krakow.

Spring.name: Szeged Open Air Festival
Spring.description: A world-renowned outdoor theater festival held at the Dóm Square, featuring opera, musicals, and plays in a stunning open-air setting.

Spring.name: Tisza River Cycling
Spring.description: Enjoy scenic cycling routes along the Tisza River as the weather warms, perfect for nature lovers and outdoor enthusiasts.

Spring.name: Spring Flower Markets
Spring.description: Explore vibrant flower markets around the city, ideal for picking up local blooms and enjoying the fresh spring air.

Spring.name: Traditional Hungarian Tisza Lajos Soup Tasting
Spring.description: Sample Tisza Lajos, a traditional spicy fish soup popular in Szeged, especially fresh during spring fishing seasons.

Summer.name: Szeged Day (Szeged Napja)
Summer.description: A lively city celebration featuring concerts, fireworks, and cultural programs to mark Szeged’s founding.

Summer.name: Tiszavirág Festival
Summer.description: An annual open-air music and cultural festival celebrating Hungarian folk, jazz, and world music by the Tisza River.

Summer.name: River Beach Swimming and Sunbathing
Summer.description: Relax and swim at the artificial river beach on the Tisza, a favorite summer spot for locals and tourists alike.

Summer.name: Szeged Paprika Festival
Summer.description: Celebrate Szeged’s famous paprika with tastings, cooking demonstrations, and market stalls selling local spices.

Autumn.name: Szeged Autumn Festival
Autumn.description: A multi-disciplinary festival offering concerts, art exhibitions, and theater performances showcasing regional talent.

Autumn.name: Tisza River Fishing
Autumn.description: Autumn is prime fishing season on the Tisza River, ideal for anglers seeking carp and catfish in cooler weather.

Autumn.name: Harvest Wine Tasting
Autumn.description: Sample freshly harvested regional wines at local wine bars and harvest festivals celebrating Szeged’s viticulture.

Autumn.name: City Park Walks in Fall Foliage
Autumn.description: Stroll the leafy streets and parks like Anna-kert to admire the colorful autumn foliage and crisp air.

Winter.name: Szeged Christmas Market
Winter.description: A charming traditional Christmas market with festive lights, crafts, mulled wine, and seasonal treats at Dóm Square.

Winter.name: Thermal Spa Visits
Winter.description: Warm up during winter at Szeged’s thermal baths, offering relaxing pools and wellness treatments year-round.

Winter.name: Winter Folk Music Concerts
Winter.description: Experience intimate folk music concerts in cozy indoor venues highlighting Hungarian winter traditions.

Winter.name: Hearty Hungarian Winter Cuisine
Winter.description: Savor warming dishes like goulash and stuffed cabbage in Szeged’s traditional eateries to beat the cold.

ranges.notes: Cold winter weather with temperatures often below freezing; low tourist activity but New Year's celebrations and Epiphany on Jan 6 provide some local events.
Coldest part of winter; very low tourist activity; many outdoor attractions less enjoyable due to weather.
Still cold, but locals begin indoor cultural events; museums and cafes are good refuge; low to moderate crowds.
Winter continues; weather still cold and sometimes snowy; good for budget travelers and business trips with fewer tourists.
Cold and sometimes snowy; off-peak season with low crowds; indoor museums and thermal baths popular.
Festival brings cultural performances and some crowds; weather still cold but increased local events improve experience.
Cold weather persists but days gradually lengthen; good for cultural sightseeing and thermal baths.
Approaching early spring; slightly warmer; low to moderate crowds; good value for budget travelers.
Early spring with gradually warming weather; outdoor sites start to open; moderate visitor numbers.
National holiday with parades and celebrations; good cultural experience but some crowding in central areas.
Spring is well underway; pleasant temperatures in mid-teens Celsius; ideal for walking tours, riverbank strolls, and early outdoor activities.
Mild spring weather with blossoming parks; comfortable for outdoor activities; moderate crowds.
Easter brings local cultural events and some visitors; weather usually pleasant; good time for cultural immersion.
Warm spring weather, ideal for exploring outdoor attractions like the Szeged Cathedral and riverside parks; crowds grow but remain manageable.
Warm, pleasant weather with temperatures around 20°C; May Day celebrations and spring festivals create lively atmosphere; excellent for all travelers.
Consistently warm, sunny spring weather perfect for outdoor dining, river walks, and sightseeing; low rainfall; crowds increase but not overwhelming.
Early summer with warm sunny days averaging 22-25°C; perfect for outdoor festivals, cycling, and river activities; moderate crowds.

ranges.event: Szeged Open Air Festival (starts late June)
ranges.notes: Festival season begins with open-air theater and music events; warm weather; lively atmosphere; crowd levels rise around events.
High summer with hot weather often reaching 30°C; peak festival season; vibrant nightlife and events; higher crowds especially during festival days.
Very hot weather can be intense; good for water activities on the Tisza river; crowds remain high but taper off slightly after festival peak.
Hot and humid summer weather continues; some locals begin vacation; good for outdoor dining and river activities; crowds moderate to high.
National holiday with fireworks and festivities; hot weather; event draws local crowds and tourists; expect higher prices and busy venues.
Early autumn with warm, pleasant weather; perfect for outdoor sightseeing and walking tours; crowds moderate and tapering.
Wine festival attracts visitors with tastings and regional food; mild weather ideal for outdoor festivals; excellent time to visit.
Autumn colors begin; cooler but still pleasant weather; fewer tourists; good for cultural visits and quiet walks.
Weather becomes cooler and wetter; outdoor activities less comfortable; fewer events; low crowds and lower prices.
Cold and damp weather; many outdoor attractions closed or less appealing; very low tourist numbers.
Cold, often foggy and rainy; low tourist appeal; indoor museums and thermal baths are best options.

ranges.event: Advent and Christmas Markets (start late November/early December)
ranges.notes: Winter cold but festive atmosphere with Christmas markets and decorations; popular among cultural travelers; moderate crowds.
Cold weather continues; Christmas markets ongoing; good for festive cultural experiences; some days may be snowy.
Holiday celebrations with markets, concerts, and festivities; cold but festive; higher prices and crowds around Christmas and New Year's Eve.
